company.name: Krystal Biotech
company.subtitle: VYJUVEK $389M 2025 · $205M net income · KB407 CF gene therapy · 4 products by 2030

market.notes: First-ever redosable gene therapy. 2025 revenue: $389M (+34% YoY), Q4: $107M. 94% gross margin. $730M cumulative since launch. 575+ reimbursement approvals. Launched in Germany, France, Japan. Expanding to 40+ countries in 2026. ~500K DEB patients globally.

us.reachPct.note: VYJUVEK DEB topical HSV gene tx approved May-2023; ~3K US DEB pts; 55% peak reach given weekly/biweekly application, broad severity; 2024 sales $291M, 2025E ~$500M.

us.priceK.note: ~$630K/yr WAC weight-based; ~$490K net after Medicaid rebates; ~$24K/vial with chronic dosing.

us.wtpPct.note: 75% -- debra + dermatology KOL strong advocacy; commercial + Medicaid cover w/ PA; first DEB therapy approved.

eu.reachPct.note: 20% -- EMA approved Apr-2025; launch Germany/UK 2025; pricing negotiations Italy/France/Spain.

eu.wtpPct.note: 40% -- HTA accepts DEB ultra-rare severe unmet need; G-BA 'considerable' expected.

row.reachPct.note: 12% -- Japan PMDA pending; KRYS building global commercial; Brazil/MENA access.

row.wtpPct.note: 22% -- Japan NHI expected 2026; MENA Gulf tier reimbursement.

us.patientsK.note: debra of America: ~3K US DEB (dystrophic EB); ~200-500 severe RDEB; COL7A1 mutation; ultra-rare.

us.priceK.note: Blended $800K: Vyjuvek $600K/yr + Zevaskyn ~$3M one-time (spread) + Filsuvez $100K + wound care $50K baseline.

eu.patientsK.note: EB-CLINET: ~4K EU27+UK DEB; ~600 severe RDEB; DEBRA International support network.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~20K DEB; consanguinity areas higher incidence; Middle East/South Asia.

us.wtpPct.note: ~80% -- Vyjuvek (beremagene geperpavec, 2023) + Filsuvez (birch bark, 2024) + Zevaskyn (pz-cel, 2025) covered; wound care supplies.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- EMA approved Filsuvez; Vyjuvek/Zevaskyn rolling out; ERN-Skin specialist centers.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- wound care + supportive; gene/cell therapy access minimal; Japan PMDA evaluating.

eu.priceK.note: ~$300K blended -- Filsuvez ~$60K + wound care + selective gene therapy access.

row.priceK.note: ~$25K -- wound care supplies + pain management; novel therapy access rare.

penPct.note: ~60% peak -- Vyjuvek/Zevaskyn/Filsuvez expanding rapidly; first disease-modifying therapies in DEB history.

market.notes: Eye drop gene therapy for DEB corneal erosions. Phase 3 registrational study ongoing. Topline data expected 2026. Same HSV-1 platform as VYJUVEK. Orphan rare disease pricing.

us.patientsK.note: DEBRA/Cure DEB: ~3K US DEB; ocular complications (corneal erosions, symblepharon) in ~500 severe RDEB.

eu.patientsK.note: EB-CLINET/DEBRA EU: ~4K EU-5 DEB; ~700 with ocular manifestations; topical Vyjuvek (Krystal) approved.

row.patientsK.note: Global DEB ~50K; ex-US/EU ~1K with severe ocular involvement accessible in specialty centers.

us.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- Vyjuvek topical gene tx approved 2023 for skin; ocular formulation Krystal B-VEC-101 P1/2.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~30% -- EMA Vyjuvek 2024; ocular indication pending; named-patient ultra-rare access.

row.wtpPct.note: ~10% -- ultra-rare ocular subset; Japan/EU-adjacent only primary ROW.

us.priceK.note: Vyjuvek ocular modeled ~$350K/yr topical redosing; Krystal pipeline indication extension.

eu.priceK.note: ~$230K/yr post-HTA ocular extension; named-patient where available.

penPct.note: ~25% peak -- severe ocular DEB subset only; Vyjuvek topical delivery feasibility dependent.

us.reachPct.note: KB803 ophthalmic DEB eye mfg Ph3 IOLITE; ~30% of DEB pts have corneal scarring; 40% peak reach as line-extension for existing VYJUVEK pts.

us.priceK.note: ~$150K/yr incremental WAC pricing; topical ocular gene tx premium.

us.wtpPct.note: 60% -- VYJUVEK pull-through; PAs straightforward given same molecule + infrastructure.

eu.reachPct.note: 15% -- EU launch 2027+; HTA benchmark vs VYJUVEK.

market.notes: NK = corneal nerve damage → loss of sensation → ulceration. Expanded enrollment from 27→60 patients. Topline before end of 2026. Competitor: Oxervate (cenegermin, Dompé) but limited efficacy.

us.patientsK.note: AAO: ~5K US neurotrophic keratitis stage 2/3 (corneal ulcer); Oxervate (cenegermin) approved 2018.

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet EU-5: ~7K NK stage 2/3; Oxervate EMA approved 2017; Dompe (now Chiesi) commercial.

row.patientsK.note: Global NK ~80K moderate-severe; ex-US/EU ~4K on specialty corneal care.

us.wtpPct.note: ~60% -- Oxervate first-in-class; strong ophthalmology KOL adoption; specialty pharmacy dispense.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- EMA approved with reimbursement; Chiesi commercial across EU-5.

row.wtpPct.note: ~20% -- Japan PMDA approved 2024; emerging markets limited specialty access.

us.priceK.note: Oxervate ~$96K per 8-week course (6x daily drops); typically 1-2 courses.

penPct.note: ~35% peak of stage 2/3 NK; 8-week course nature limits chronic exposure.

us.reachPct.note: KB801 neurotrophic keratitis Ph1/2; ~65K US NK pts; 25% peak reach (2028+) vs Oxervate (cenegermin).

us.wtpPct.note: 45% -- ophthalmology KOL open to gene tx vs weekly cenegermin drops; payors will compare cost-effectiveness.

eu.reachPct.note: 10% -- EU launch 2029+; EMA rare ophth path.

assets.name: KB407 — inhaled gene therapy (Cystic Fibrosis)
assets.stage: Phase 1 (CORAL-1 Cohort 3 positive, CORAL-3 repeat dose planned)

market.notes: Inhaled redosable gene therapy delivering full-length CFTR. CORAL-1 Cohort 3: 29-42% airway transduction confirmed. Works regardless of mutation type — unlike Trikafta (only ~90% of CF patients). CORAL-3 repeat dose study design submitted to FDA. If repeat dosing works: paradigm shift — gene therapy for CF that works in ALL patients.

us.wtpPct.note: Rare disease default -- strong commercial payor coverage analog (75%).

row.wtpPct.note: Rare disease default -- limited reimbursement ex-Japan (15%).

us.priceK.note: US list price anchor -- typically ~1.25x global avg for rare.

eu.priceK.note: EU rare-disease net price -- ~55% of US list after HTA discount.

row.priceK.note: Blended ROW -- Japan near EU, emerging far lower (~20% of US).

penPct.note: Peak penetration inherited from legacy config -- refine per competitive landscape.

us.reachPct.note: KB407 CF inhaled HSV gene tx Ph1; ~10% CF pts non-modulator eligible; 15% peak reach in that subset (2030+) vs Trikafta ineligible.

us.wtpPct.note: 35% -- niche CF subpop; payors cautious on inhaled gene tx durability vs Trikafta $300K/yr.

market.notes: Redosable inhaled immunotherapy for NSCLC. RMAT designation (Oct 2025). Early stage but enormous TAM. HSV-1 vector delivers IL-12 directly to tumor microenvironment.

us.patientsK.note: ~8K US RET/NTRK/EGFR ex20/BRAF NSCLC/yr combined; rare driver mix. Source: NCI + oncogenic driver consortium.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~10K combined rare drivers; ESMO reflex NGS captures most.

us.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- Retevmo RET+, Rozlytrek NTRK+, Rybrevant EGFR ex20+, Tafinlar+Mekinist BRAF V600E+ all NCCN Cat 1.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~48% -- NICE + G-BA approved selpercatinib + entrectinib + amivantamab.

row.wtpPct.note: ~16% -- Japan PMDA full; China NRDL selective.

us.priceK.note: Blended $210K: RET TKI $225K (25% pts) + NTRK TKI $225K (10%) + Rybrevant EGFR ex20 $240K (30%) + BRAF combo $220K (15%) + chemo $30K (20%).

penPct.note: ~55% peak -- tgt rx for rare drivers at high WTP; next-gen (zipalertinib EGFR ex20, NVL-520 RET) extend 5yr.

us.reachPct.note: KB707 IL-2/IL-12 HSV oncolytic intratumoral NSCLC Ph1; 10% peak reach in checkpoint-refractory solid tumors (2030+).

us.wtpPct.note: 30% -- crowded oncolytic space (T-VEC precedent limited); payors will demand OS benefit.

market.notes: Rare genetic skin disease. Fast Track designation. Topical gene therapy. Orphan pricing. HSV-1 platform extension.

us.patientsK.note: NORD: ~2K US Hailey-Hailey Disease; ~1:50K prevalence; ATP2C1 mutation; autosomal dominant.

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet: ~3K EU27+UK Hailey-Hailey; intertriginous erosions; heat/friction triggers.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~15K Hailey-Hailey; underdiagnosed; often misdiagnosed as candidiasis or intertrigo.

us.wtpPct.note: ~30% -- no FDA-approved therapy; off-label topical steroids + antibiotics + botulinum toxin + laser.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~25% -- no EMA approval; off-label same as US; dermabrasion/laser specialist centers.

row.wtpPct.note: ~8% -- topical steroids + antibiotics; specialist access limited.

us.priceK.note: Blended $3K: off-label TCS/TCI generic $200 + antibiotics $500 + botulinum toxin $2K/session + laser procedures $3-5K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$1.5K blended -- off-label generics + selective procedural interventions.

penPct.note: ~10% peak -- no approved therapy; Notch pathway + topical JAK pipeline early-stage; niche specialty.

us.reachPct.note: KB111 Hailey-Hailey disease topical HSV gene tx; ~40K US HHD; 35% peak reach (2029+) as first HHD therapy.

us.priceK.note: ~$300K/yr WAC HHD ultra-rare pricing parallel to DEB.

us.wtpPct.note: 50% -- derm KOL enthusiasm; payors cover as orphan genodermatosis.

eu.reachPct.note: 12% -- EU HHD dx registry strong; HTA accepts orphan.

mega_bear.narrative: VYJUVEK growth stalls + CF gene therapy fails + platform limited to skin
events: ✗ VYJUVEK revenue plateaus at ~$450M — DEB market ceiling hit
✗ KB407 CF repeat dosing shows immune response — gene therapy can't redose in lungs
✗ KB803 and KB801 ophthalmic programs miss
→ Single-product rare disease company at $450M peak — overvalued at $7.6B

bear.narrative: VYJUVEK grows modestly + ophthalmology approved but small + CF delayed
events: ✓ VYJUVEK grows to ~$550M but global penetration slower than expected
✓ KB803/KB801 approved — small orphan additions
✗ KB407 CF needs more dose optimization — CORAL-3 delayed
→ 3 marketed products but CF not yet contributing

base.narrative: 4 marketed products by 2030 + CF gene therapy advancing + profitable growth
events: ✓ VYJUVEK grows to $700M+ with global expansion to 40+ countries
✓ KB803 + KB801 approved — rare disease franchise in derm + ophthalmology
✓ KB407 CF CORAL-3 repeat dosing shows durable CFTR expression
~ KB707 NSCLC early data generates oncology interest

bull.narrative: Gene therapy platform leader — DEB + CF + ophthalmology + oncology — $2B+ revenue by 2030
events: ✓ VYJUVEK >$1B global peak — dominant in DEB worldwide
✓ KB407 CF redosable gene therapy works — Trikafta alternative for ALL mutations
✓ 4+ marketed products, profitable, growing 30%+ annually
✓ Platform technology designation enables accelerated paths for new indications

psychedelic_bull.narrative: Redosable gene therapy platform redefines genetic medicine — Vertex/Roche acquires at $500+
events: ✓ VYJUVEK $1.5B+ global — gene therapy standard for skin diseases
✓ KB407 cures CF regardless of mutation — replaces Trikafta ($10B franchise)
✓ KB707 NSCLC immunotherapy validated — inhaled IO new paradigm
✓ Vertex or Roche acquires for $15-20B to own redosable gene therapy platform

lastData.title: VYJUVEK $389M 2025, $205M net income, $956M cash
points: KB407 CF CORAL-1 Cohort 3: 29-42% airway transduction
KB801 NK registrational study expanded 27→60 patients
Goal: 4 marketed rare disease products by 2030

nextCatalyst.title: KB803 ocular DEB Phase 3 + KB801 NK topline
thresholds: KB801 NK registrational topline before end of 2026
KB407 CF CORAL-3 repeat dose study design/initiation

risks: DEB is a small market — VYJUVEK revenue ceiling may be $800M-1B
$7.6B market cap at 19x 2025 revenue — richly valued
KB407 CF: redosing in lungs is scientifically unproven — immune clearance risk
KB707 NSCLC is very early and speculative
Gene therapy manufacturing complex and expensive
Trikafta franchise ($10B) means Vertex will compete aggressively if KB407 works
Stock has 3x'd since 2021 — expectations are high

catalysts.title: CORAL-3 CF registrational study alignment with FDA
catalysts._rationale: Expected alignment on CORAL-3 registrational study design with FDA in Q1 2026

catalysts.title: KB111 Hailey-Hailey disease Phase 1/2 first patient dosed
catalysts._rationale: Company expects to initiate registrational study in 2H 2026 after completing development and validation of HHD-specific evaluation scale in 1H 2026

catalysts.title: KB803 ocular DEB Phase 3 registrational topline
catalysts._rationale: Company expects to complete enrollment in 1H 2026 and report top-line results before year end

catalysts.title: KB801 neurotrophic keratitis registrational topline
catalysts._rationale: Registrational data readouts for ophthalmology programs KB801 expected before the end of 2026
